Title: Structure, function and assembly of soybean primary cell wall cellulose synthases

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary structural component of the plant primary cell wall; contributes to cell wall mechanics and wall material deposition
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes glucose from UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan cellulose and releases UDP
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase family; GT2 glycosyltransferase; plant conserved region (PCR); class-specific region (CSR); QxxRW motif
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Membrane protein with transmembrane cellulose-conducting channel; plasma membrane-associated cellulose synthase inferred
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely and strongly expressed in unlignified soybean tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Widely and strongly expressed in unlignified soybean tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assays; catalytic optimum at neutral to mild-alkaline pH, peaking near pH 8; UDP competitively inhibits activity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; GmCesA1 showed the highest in vitro cellulose-producing activity among the three isoforms
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalytically active cellulose synthase that directly produces cellulose; exhibits the highest in vitro activity of the three isoforms and shows synergistically increased cellulose synthesis when combined with different CesA isoforms
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Purified GmCesA1 forms stable homotrimers and monomers, both catalytically active in vitro. 2. GmCesA1 synthesizes authentic cellulase-sensitive cellulose from UDP-glucose and shows peak activity around pH 8. 3. GmCesA1 has higher apparent catalytic output than GmCesA3 and GmCesA6. 4. GmCesA1 trimers interact with GmCesA3 and GmCesA6, but not detectably with GmCesA1 trimers of the same isoform. 5. Cross-isoform association causes synergistic cellulose biosynthesis above additive expectations. 6. The CSR is required for inter-isoform interaction, whereas the NTD is dispensable.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression; protein purification; size exclusion chromatography; cryo-EM; radiometric cellulose synthesis assay with UDP-[3H]-Glc; cellulase digestion; pH profiling; UDP inhibition assay; tandem affinity pull-down; truncation/deletion analysis; synergy assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-expression analysis using ATTED-II v11; structural data: RCSB PDB 8VHZ
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering cellulose deposition and primary wall properties; likely best considered in isoform combinations rather than singly because cross-isoform interactions enhance catalytic output
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homotrimers were structurally resolved; hetero-oligomeric complexes were not isolated. CSR-mediated inter-trimer interaction, not same-isoform trimer association, is supported. Apparent Km for UDP-Glc ~0.44 mM; UDP IC50 about 0.8 mM. Monomeric fraction was also catalytically active in vitro.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary structural component of the plant primary cell wall; contributes to cell wall mechanics and wall material deposition
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes glucose from UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan cellulose and releases UDP
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase family; GT2 glycosyltransferase; PCR; CSR; QxxRW motif
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Membrane protein with transmembrane cellulose-conducting channel; plasma membrane-associated cellulose synthase inferred
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely and strongly expressed in unlignified soybean tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Widely and strongly expressed in unlignified soybean tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assays; catalytic optimum at pH 7 with decline at pH 8-9; UDP inhibits activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalytically active cellulose synthase that directly produces cellulose and displays synergistically increased cellulose synthesis when combined with different CesA isoforms
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Purified GmCesA3 forms stable homotrimers and monomers, both catalytically active in vitro. 2. GmCesA3 synthesizes authentic cellulase-sensitive cellulose from UDP-glucose and has an activity optimum at pH 7. 3. GmCesA3 trimers interact with GmCesA1 and GmCesA6, but not detectably with GmCesA3 trimers of the same isoform. 4. Deletion analysis shows the CSR is required for inter-isoform interaction, whereas the NTD is dispensable. 5. Cross-isoform interaction produces synergistic cellulose biosynthesis above additive expectations.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression; protein purification; size exclusion chromatography; cryo-EM; radiometric cellulose synthesis assay with UDP-[3H]-Glc; cellulase digestion; pH profiling; UDP-Glo kinetics; UDP inhibition assay; tandem affinity pull-down; truncation/deletion analysis; synergy assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-expression analysis using ATTED-II v11; structural data: RCSB PDB 8VHT
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for combinatorial engineering of cellulose deposition and wall material properties through isoform-aware design
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Apparent Km for UDP-Glc ~0.18 mM; apparent Vmax substantially lower than GmCesA1; UDP IC50 about 1.3-1.5 mM. Cellobiose was modeled at acceptor position in cryo-EM structure.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary structural component of the plant primary cell wall; contributes to cell wall mechanics and wall material deposition
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes glucose from UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan cellulose and releases UDP
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase family; GT2 glycosyltransferase; PCR; CSR; QxxRW motif
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Membrane protein with transmembrane cellulose-conducting channel; plasma membrane-associated cellulose synthase inferred
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely and strongly expressed in unlignified soybean tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Widely and strongly expressed in unlignified soybean tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assays; catalytic optimum near pH 8; UDP inhibits activity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; lower activity and less stable trimer yield than GmCesA1 under detergent-solubilized conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalytically active cellulose synthase that directly produces cellulose and displays synergistically increased cellulose synthesis when combined with different CesA isoforms
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Purified GmCesA6 forms catalytically active homotrimers and monomers in vitro. 2. GmCesA6 synthesizes authentic cellulase-sensitive cellulose from UDP-glucose and shows peak activity near pH 8. 3. Trimeric GmCesA6 is less stable in detergent-solubilized state than GmCesA1 and GmCesA3. 4. GmCesA6 trimers interact with GmCesA1 and GmCesA3, but not detectably with GmCesA6 trimers of the same isoform. 5. Cross-isoform association enhances cellulose biosynthesis synergistically above additive expectations.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression; protein purification; size exclusion chromatography; cryo-EM; radiometric cellulose synthesis assay with UDP-[3H]-Glc; cellulase digestion; pH profiling; UDP inhibition assay; tandem affinity pull-down; synergy assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-expression analysis using ATTED-II v11; structural data: RCSB PDB 8VI0
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Trimeric protein was less stable and more variably recovered than GmCesA1 and GmCesA3 in detergent-solubilized preparations
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant component for multiplex engineering of primary wall cellulose deposition, especially in combination with other primary-wall CesA isoforms
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Activity was too weak for UDP-Glo kinetic analysis under conditions used. UDP IC50 about 1.3 mM. Cellobiose was modeled at acceptor position in cryo-EM structure.
